name: OpenClaw Gateway Token Theft
description: OpenClaw Control UI trusts gatewayUrl query parameter without validation, allowing token theft via crafted links.
remediation: Update OpenClaw to version 2026.1.29 or later. Validate gateway URLs before connecting.

name: ClawHub Credential Stealer -- env-dump skill
description: Malicious ClawHub skill that exfiltrates all environment variables including API keys and tokens to a remote endpoint.
remediation: Remove the skill immediately. Rotate all API keys and tokens that were present in the environment.

name: ClawHub Credential Stealer -- keychain-read skill
description: Malicious ClawHub skill that reads system keychain entries and exfiltrates stored credentials.
remediation: Remove the skill immediately. Change all passwords stored in the system keychain.

name: ClawHub Credential Stealer -- ssh-key-grab skill
description: Malicious ClawHub skill that reads and exfiltrates SSH private keys from the user home directory.
remediation: Remove the skill immediately. Regenerate all SSH keys and revoke compromised public keys from authorized hosts.

name: ClawHub Env Exfiltrator -- dotenv-sync skill
description: Malicious ClawHub skill that searches for .env files across the filesystem and uploads their contents to a remote server.
remediation: Remove the skill. Rotate all secrets found in .env files on the system.

name: Supply Chain -- Suspicious npm package (hack pattern)
description: MCP server launched via npm package with suspicious name containing 'hack'. Likely malicious supply chain package.
remediation: Remove the MCP server configuration. Verify the package source on npmjs.com before reinstalling.

name: Supply Chain -- Suspicious npm package (exfil pattern)
description: MCP server launched via npm package with suspicious name containing 'exfil'. Likely a data exfiltration tool.
remediation: Remove the MCP server configuration immediately. Audit system for signs of data exfiltration.

name: Supply Chain -- Suspicious npm package (backdoor pattern)
description: MCP server launched via npm package with suspicious name containing 'backdoor' or 'pwn'. Likely contains a remote access backdoor.
remediation: Remove the MCP server configuration immediately. Scan system for persistence mechanisms. Consider reimaging the host.

name: RCE via Command Injection -- semicolon in args
description: MCP server arguments contain shell command injection via semicolon separator. Attacker can execute arbitrary commands.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Never pass untrusted input as command arguments. Sanitize all args for shell metacharacters.

name: RCE via Command Injection -- pipe in args
description: MCP server arguments contain shell command injection via pipe operator. Attacker can chain arbitrary commands.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Audit argument handling for shell injection vulnerabilities.

name: RCE via Command Injection -- backtick or subshell in args
description: MCP server arguments contain shell command injection via subshell or backtick execution. Attacker can execute arbitrary commands.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Ensure arguments are never passed through a shell interpreter.

name: Dangerous Tool -- Unrestricted filesystem read access
description: MCP server requests unrestricted read access to the entire filesystem. This can expose sensitive files including credentials and private keys.
remediation: Restrict filesystem access to specific directories. Use the principle of least privilege for file access permissions.

name: Dangerous Tool -- Unrestricted network access
description: MCP server requests unrestricted network access. This can be used to exfiltrate data or establish reverse shells.
remediation: Restrict network access to specific hosts and ports. Monitor outbound connections from MCP servers.

name: Dangerous Tool -- Process execution without justification
description: MCP server has unrestricted process execution capability. This bypasses all sandboxing and allows arbitrary command execution.
remediation: Restrict execution to specific binaries. Use allowlists for permitted commands.

name: Dangerous Tool -- Clipboard and screenshot access
description: MCP server accesses clipboard contents or captures screenshots. This can expose sensitive data copied by the user.
remediation: Remove the server unless clipboard/screenshot access is explicitly required. Monitor for data exfiltration.

name: Docker Escape -- Privileged container mount
description: MCP server arguments request privileged Docker access or host PID namespace. This enables container escape and full host compromise.
remediation: Never run MCP servers with --privileged flag. Remove Docker socket mounts. Use rootless containers.

name: Docker Escape -- Host filesystem mount
description: MCP server mounts the host root filesystem or sensitive host directories into a container. This allows reading and modifying host system files.
remediation: Restrict volume mounts to specific application directories. Never mount / or /etc into containers.

name: Docker Escape -- CAP_SYS_ADMIN capability
description: MCP server container requests SYS_ADMIN or ALL capabilities. This grants near-root privileges and enables container escape.
remediation: Drop all capabilities and add back only what is strictly needed. Use --cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=<specific>.

name: Docker Escape -- Host network namespace
description: MCP server container uses host network namespace. This bypasses network isolation and exposes all host network interfaces.
remediation: Use bridge or custom Docker networks instead of host networking. Restrict container network access.

name: Docker Escape -- Cgroup escape via release_agent
description: MCP server arguments reference cgroup release_agent mechanism. This is a known container escape technique.
remediation: Remove the MCP server immediately. Audit the host for signs of compromise. Use cgroup v2 with restricted permissions.

name: mcp-remote RCE via OAuth URL injection
description: mcp-remote versions before 0.4.0 pass malicious OAuth endpoint URLs directly to the shell, enabling arbitrary OS command execution when connecting to untrusted servers. Affected 437,000+ downloads.
remediation: Update mcp-remote to version 0.4.0 or later. Never connect to untrusted MCP servers with vulnerable versions.

name: Figma MCP Server command injection
description: Critical command injection vulnerability in the Figma MCP server affecting 600,000+ downloads. Attacker-controlled input is passed unsanitized to shell execution.
remediation: Update to the latest patched version of the Figma MCP server. Validate all input parameters before shell execution.

name: mcp-atlassian unauthenticated RCE and SSRF
description: Critical unauthenticated RCE and SSRF via path traversal in Confluence attachment downloads. Allows arbitrary file write and internal network access.
remediation: Update mcp-atlassian to the latest patched version. Restrict network access and validate all file paths.

name: Anthropic filesystem server path validation bypass
description: Path validation bypass in official Anthropic filesystem MCP server. When chained with git server vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-68143, CVE-2025-68144), enables full RCE via malicious .git/config files.
remediation: Update to the latest version of the filesystem server. Restrict allowed directories to the minimum needed.

name: Anthropic git server argument injection via git_diff
description: Argument injection in git_diff tool and unrestricted git_init in official Anthropic git MCP server. Combined with filesystem server, enables RCE through crafted .git/config.
remediation: Update to the latest version of the git server. Sanitize all arguments passed to git commands.

name: Azure MCP Server SSRF via OAuth proxy
description: SSRF vulnerability in Azure MCP Server. Trusts X-Atlassian headers and OAuth proxy requests without authorization boundary validation, enabling unauthorized access to internal services.
remediation: Update Azure MCP Server to the latest version. Implement strict authorization boundaries for OAuth proxy requests.

name: NeighborJack -- MCP server bound to 0.0.0.0
description: MCP server explicitly bound to all network interfaces (0.0.0.0). Any device on the same local network can access the server without authentication, enabling unauthorized tool execution.
remediation: Bind MCP servers to 127.0.0.1 (localhost) only. Use firewall rules to restrict access. Add authentication to all exposed endpoints.

name: Supply Chain -- Malicious PyPI MCP package (reverse shell)
description: Malicious MCP server package from PyPI containing reverse shell functionality. Establishes outbound connection to attacker-controlled infrastructure upon installation.
remediation: Remove the package immediately. Check for active reverse shell connections. Audit system for persistence mechanisms and compromised credentials.

name: Supply Chain -- Suspicious pip MCP package (exfil/hack/backdoor)
description: MCP server installed via pip/pipx with suspicious package name indicating malicious intent. Python MCP packages on PyPI have been used to deploy reverse shells and data exfiltration tools.
remediation: Remove the package immediately. Verify package source and author on PyPI. Scan system for signs of compromise.

name: Postmark MCP Server impersonation (email exfiltration)
description: Impersonation of legitimate Postmark email service MCP server. Injects BCC copies of all sent emails to attacker-controlled addresses, exfiltrating confidential documents, memos, and invoices.
remediation: Remove the server immediately. Verify the package source against the official Postmark documentation. Audit sent emails for unauthorized BCC recipients.

name: SSRF via MarkItDown MCP Server (cloud metadata theft)
description: SSRF vulnerability in MarkItDown MCP server affecting 36.7% of analyzed cloud deployments. Enables exploitation of cloud metadata services (AWS EC2 169.254.169.254) to steal IAM credentials and access tokens.
remediation: Update to the latest patched version. Block access to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254). Run MCP servers with IMDSv2 enforcement on AWS.

name: Azure Data Explorer KQL injection
description: KQL (Kusto Query Language) injection vulnerability in Azure Data Explorer MCP server. Unvalidated query parameters enable data exfiltration and unauthorized query execution.
remediation: Update to the latest version. Parameterize all KQL queries. Implement query validation and sandboxing.

name: RCE via base64-encoded command injection in args
description: MCP server arguments contain base64-encoded command injection. Attacker obfuscates malicious commands using base64 encoding to evade simple pattern detection.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Never pass base64-decoded content directly to shell execution. Audit all argument handling for injection vulnerabilities.

name: Claude Code WebSocket authentication bypass
description: Authentication bypass in Claude Code IDE extensions via WebSocket. Allows unauthenticated local connections to execute tools with the user's full privileges.
remediation: Update Claude Code extensions to the latest version. Ensure WebSocket connections require authentication tokens.

name: Supply Chain -- Suspicious npm MCP package (shell/exec pattern)
description: MCP server launched via npm package with name indicating unrestricted shell or command execution capability. These packages often grant full system access without sandboxing.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Verify the package source and audit its code before use. Prefer MCP servers with explicit permission scoping.

name: WhatsApp MCP Server chat history exfiltration
description: Tool poisoning vulnerability in WhatsApp MCP server enables attackers to inject malicious instructions that exfiltrate entire chat histories via crafted tool descriptions.
remediation: Update to the latest patched version. Review tool descriptions for hidden instructions. Restrict MCP server access to specific chat scopes.

name: Supabase MCP SQL injection via support tickets
description: SQL injection attack vector in Supabase MCP server. Attackers embed SQL instructions in support tickets that agents process, enabling exfiltration of integration tokens and database credentials.
remediation: Update to the latest version. Use parameterized queries. Sanitize all user-provided content before passing to MCP tools. Restrict database permissions to read-only where possible.

name: Anthropic git server unrestricted git_init (CVE-2025-68143)
description: Unrestricted git_init tool in official Anthropic git MCP server can turn sensitive directories (e.g. .ssh) into git repositories, enabling exfiltration of private keys via git operations.
remediation: Update to the latest version of the git server. Restrict git_init to approved directories only.

name: Supply Chain -- Typosquatted MCP npm package
description: MCP server installed via npm package with a name resembling common typos of legitimate MCP packages. Typosquatting is a known supply chain attack vector for distributing malware through package registries.
remediation: Verify the exact package name against the official repository. Remove any typosquatted packages immediately. Run npm audit to check for known vulnerabilities.

name: Supply Chain -- Typosquatted MCP PyPI package
description: MCP server installed via pip with a name resembling common typos of legitimate MCP packages. Typosquatting on PyPI has been used to distribute reverse shells and credential stealers targeting MCP users.
remediation: Verify the exact package name against the official PyPI listing. Remove any typosquatted packages. Check for active reverse shell connections.

name: HTTP transport without TLS for remote MCP server
description: MCP server configured to use unencrypted HTTP transport. All tool invocations, credentials, and data are transmitted in plaintext, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks and credential interception.
remediation: Switch to HTTPS for all remote MCP server connections. Enable TLS certificate validation. Use mTLS for high-security environments.

name: RCE via Command Injection -- ampersand chaining in args
description: MCP server arguments contain shell command injection via ampersand operator chaining. Attacker can chain arbitrary commands after the intended command completes.
remediation: Remove the MCP server. Sanitize all arguments for shell metacharacters including &, &&, and other chaining operators.

name: Docker Escape -- seccomp disabled
description: MCP server container runs with seccomp or AppArmor security profiles disabled. This removes kernel-level syscall filtering and mandatory access controls, enabling container escape.
remediation: Remove --security-opt overrides. Use the default seccomp and AppArmor profiles. Apply custom restrictive profiles if the default is too permissive.

name: Slack MCP Server message exfiltration
description: Slack MCP server with overly broad OAuth scopes can be exploited to exfiltrate channel messages, DMs, and files. Tool poisoning can inject instructions to silently forward messages to attacker-controlled channels.
remediation: Restrict OAuth scopes to minimum required permissions. Review tool descriptions for hidden exfiltration instructions. Monitor Slack API usage for anomalous access patterns.

name: GitHub MCP Server token scope abuse
description: GitHub MCP server configured with overly broad personal access token scopes. Tool poisoning can exploit write permissions to push malicious code, create backdoor branches, or modify CI/CD workflows.
remediation: Use fine-grained personal access tokens with minimal scopes. Restrict to specific repositories. Prefer GitHub Apps with installation-level permissions over PATs.

name: Database MCP Server SQL injection via tool parameters
description: Database MCP servers that accept raw SQL queries from LLM agents without parameterization. Prompt injection can manipulate the agent into executing DROP, DELETE, or data exfiltration queries.
remediation: Use parameterized queries exclusively. Restrict database user to read-only permissions. Implement query allowlists. Never grant DDL privileges to MCP server database users.

name: Supply Chain -- npm install script execution
description: MCP server installation explicitly enables npm lifecycle scripts. Malicious packages use postinstall scripts to execute arbitrary code during installation, before any security review can occur.
remediation: Use --ignore-scripts when installing untrusted MCP packages. Audit package.json scripts before installation. Use npm audit to check for known vulnerabilities.

name: Cloud metadata SSRF via MCP tool parameters
description: MCP server arguments reference cloud metadata service endpoints. This enables SSRF attacks to steal IAM credentials, access tokens, and instance metadata from AWS, GCP, or Alibaba Cloud environments.
remediation: Block access to cloud metadata endpoints at the network level. Enforce IMDSv2 on AWS. Run MCP servers in isolated network segments without metadata service access.

name: MCP server with OAuth static client secret
description: MCP server configured with a static OAuth client secret in command-line arguments. Secrets in process arguments are visible via /proc and process listing, enabling credential theft.
remediation: Use environment variables or secure credential stores for OAuth secrets. Implement PKCE for public clients. Rotate compromised client secrets immediately.

name: MCP server with disabled certificate validation
description: MCP server configured to skip TLS certificate validation. This enables man-in-the-middle attacks where attackers can intercept and modify all traffic between the client and server, including credentials and tool responses.
remediation: Enable TLS certificate validation. Use trusted CA certificates. If using self-signed certificates, configure the specific CA bundle rather than disabling validation entirely.
